motions.title: Public Health Services Budget for 2026 Approved
motions.summary: The 2026 operating budget for the Ottawa Board of Health was approved, ensuring continued funding for essential public health programs and services in the city.
motions.full_text: The 2026 Ottawa Board of Health Draft Operating Budget as recommended by the Ottawa Board of Health; Carried

motions.title: Library Budgets for 2026 Adopted, Supporting Services and Facilities
motions.summary: The Ottawa Public Library's operating and capital budgets for 2026 were approved with a technical accounting adjustment that has no net financial impact. This decision ensures funding for library operations, programs, and facility upgrades for the coming year.
motions.full_text: The 2026 Ottawa Public Library Board Draft Operating and Capital Budgets as recommended by the Ottawa Public Library Board; Carried as amended

motions.title: Police Service Budget for 2026 Approved by Council
motions.summary: Council approved the 2026 operating and capital budgets for the Ottawa Police Service. This decision provides the necessary funding for police operations, staffing, and equipment to maintain public safety.
motions.full_text: The 2026 Ottawa Police Service Draft Operating and Capital Budgets, as recommended by the Ottawa Police Services Board; Carried (20 to 5)

motions.title: City to Invest Additional $20 Million in Roads, Sidewalks, Parks & Recreation
motions.summary: Council approved an extra $20 million for core infrastructure projects such as roads, sidewalks, parks, and recreational facilities. This funding comes from a new city loan (debenture) that will be paid for by additional property assessment growth, and also includes releasing developer fees (Cash-in-lieu of parklands) for recreation projects.
motions.full_text: THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED THAT Council support the release of CILs as identified by Legal Services for the advancement of recreation infrastructure projects; and THEREFORE, BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT Council direct staff to increase the draft 2026 capital budget for Tax supported assets with an additional $20 million debenture in which the annual debt servicing of approximately $1.2 million will be funded from the incremental $1.2 million assessment growth funding; and THEREFORE, IT BE FURTHER RESOLVED THAT staff apply the $20 million capital funding towards core tax supported infrastructure such as roads, sidewalks, parks and recreational facilities. Carried

motions.title: Rural and Agricultural Programs and Capital Projects Funded for 2026
motions.summary: The operating and capital budgets for the Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee (ARAC) were approved for 2026. This decision ensures continued funding for services and infrastructure improvements in Ottawa's rural areas.
motions.full_text: THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED that Council, sitting as Committee of the Whole, approve the Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee Capital Program (page 5) [individual projects listed pages 10-18], including revised page 17 of the ARAC budget book. Carried

motions.title: Funding for Children's Services and Childcare Wage Enhancement Approved
motions.summary: Council approved specific funding within the Community Services budget for Children's Services, targeting wage enhancements for childcare workers and supporting the Canada-Wide Early Learning and Child Care (CWELCC) system. This aims to strengthen the childcare workforce and improve access to affordable childcare.
motions.full_text: Community and Social Services Department - Children's Services Operating Resource Requirement, 'Wage Enhancement & Workforce Comp' and 'Canada Wide System (CWELCC)' line items (Page 10 of the CSC Budget Book); Carried

motions.title: 'Play Free' Program Made Permanent, Offering Free Weekend Recreation
motions.summary: Ottawa City Council approved $550,000 in ongoing funding to make the popular 'Play Free' program permanent. This initiative provides free access to recreational activities like swimming, skating, and gym sports every Saturday for children and youth aged 17 and under, along with accompanying adults. The funding will come from projected savings in the city's fuel hedging program.
motions.full_text: THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT Ottawa City Council allocate $550,000 of base funding to make this program a permanent program moving forward and to: 1. Endorse and promote the Play Free program as a key initiative to encourage physical activity and family engagement across the city; 2. Direct staff to ensure clear communication and outreach regarding program details, including eligibility, and participating locations; 3. Encourage partnerships with community organizations and schools to maximize awareness and participation; and BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT the funding source for these allocations be the $550,000 in projected savings from the City's fuel hedging program reflecting updated hedging pricing and current economic analysis for 2026. Carried
